svg.text: Define recipes → system tracks theoretical food cost. Compare actual vs theoretical to detect waste, theft, portioning errors.
Food cost data is incomplete for 33 menu items.
Actual vs theoretical comparison will be skewed.
Click to expand — food cost incomplete
Recommend menu repricing or portion review
Food cost data is incomplete. Actual vs
Adjust waste % per ingredient or investigate
pos_recipes: menu_item_id → ingredients [{inventory_item_id, qty, unit, waste_pct}]
Theoretical cost = Σ(qty × unit_cost × (1 + waste_pct)) | Batch: auto-scales to order qty
Actual cost: month_end received POs − month_end count = goods used
Variance = actual − theoretical | Positive variance = over-spend (waste/theft/portioning)
Depletion: each pos_order_item confirmed → deduct recipe quantities from pos_inventory
Unit cost: last PO price from pos_po_items (updated on each receive — reflects market fluctuation)
AI recipe suggest: Qwen3-8B on RunPod estimates typical recipe for a menu item name & description
Prime cost = food cost + labor cost | Labor: (total_time_min / 60) × kitchen_hourly_wage
Food cost alert: fires when actual − theoretical exceeds variance_alert_threshold (configurable, default 3%)
Recommended sell price for target FC%: portion_cost / target_food_cost_pct
